mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports higher equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS growth over 1-month forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 on a 3-year rolling basis, or the signal is not statistically significant above noise (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points from its 12-month low, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and earnings expectations, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3pp from its 12-month minimum
failure_condition: In at least 60% of triggered instances over the past 20 years, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the average return conditional on trigger is not significantly different from unconditional mean (t-test p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 4% and housing starts exceed 1.35 million, the combined signal of easy monetary policy and robust housing demand indicates a strong cyclical upswing, pushing equity prices higher over a 20-day horizon.
regime_hint: monetary easing combined with housing demand pickup
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS × HOUST) does not produce a statistically significant coefficient in a linear regression predicting 20-day SPX returns (p > 0.05), or the mean return conditional on both conditions is not above the unconditional mean by at least 200 bps annualized.

mechanism_narrative: In the regime of post-disruption supply normalization, a sustained 3-month rise in industrial production signals that capacity constraints are easing, which relieves cost pressures and boosts equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases by more than 2% over the trailing 3 months
regime_hint: post-disruption supply normalization (e.g., after natural disaster, pandemic, or trade disruption)
failure_condition: The conditional return following the trigger shows negative average 20-day SPX returns over at least two distinct post-disruption episodes, or the signal fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold strategy over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the monthly CPI inflation rate accelerates above 0.3% (annualized >3.6%), it raises fears of tighter monetary policy and compresses equity price-earnings ratios, leading to negative short-term stock returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The average 5-day SPX return after high CPI prints is not negative, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55% over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) and robust retail sales (RSAFS) signal a virtuous cycle of employment gains fueling consumption, which amplifies corporate earnings growth and drives equity prices higher over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 150,000 AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: The joint occurrence does not produce a statistically significant positive average 60-day SPX return (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10) or the return is lower than the sum of individual signals, indicating a subadditive interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly changes in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate expanding factory output, which is quickly priced into equity markets as a sign of economic health, leading to positive returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and 5-day forward SPX returns is not statistically significant at the 5% level over any 5-year period, or the hit rate of positive returns following positive INDPRO is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: During the early stage of economic recovery from a recession, each quarter of positive GDP growth (GDPC1) reinforces expectations of sustained expansion, lifting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: early stage of economic recovery (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change turns positive after at least one quarter of negative growth)
failure_condition: The positive return signal fails to materialize in two consecutive recovery episodes since 2000, or the average 60-day SPX return conditional on the regime is not significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).
